channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Soldier who died in Las Vegas explosion left note saying it was to be a wakeup for country's ills
item.description: A highly decorated Army soldier who died in an explosion of a Tesla Cybertruck at the Trump hotel in Las Vegas left a note saying it was stunt to serve as “wakeup call” for the country’s ills, investigators said Friday.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Soldier who died by suicide in Las Vegas told ex-girlfriend of pain and exhaustion after Afghanistan
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The highly decorated Special Forces soldier who died by suicide in a Cybertruck explosion on New Years Day confided to a former girlfriend — who had served as an Army nurse — that he faced significant pain and exhaustion that she says were key symptoms of traumatic brain injury.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but promised not to jail him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Did you know? These winter driving 'tips' are outdated and unnecessary
item.description: Driving in the winter is a different beast, especially when some of the most common advice for driving in the snow and cold isn't always right.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, NewsNation

item.title: Former employee charged with child abuse after babies found with 'unexplainable fractures' at Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s NICU
item.description: A former employee of Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s neonatal intensive care unit (NICU) has been charged with malicious wounding and child abuse after an investigation into babies being found with "unexplainable fractures" at the NICU.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-employee charged after babies found with 'unexplainable fractures' at Virginia hospital
item.description: The charges comes after the hospital said three babies were discovered with "unexplainable fractures" in late November and December of 2024. A similar incident took place the previous year.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, ABC 4

item.title: Finalized tax credit for cleaner hydrogen gets a cautious OK from some environmental groups
item.description: The Biden administration released long-awaited final rules Friday for a tax credit that will send billions of dollars to producers of cleaner hydrogen.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Mcdermott, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Family searching for owner of missing urn after finding it in an Uber
item.description: It's not uncommon for a phone or wallet to be left behind in a ride-share service, but one man found an urn.
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, NewsNation

item.title: Biden honors leaders of Jan. 6 committee with civilian award
item.description: President Joe Biden awarded the Presidential Citizens Medal to 20 people, including leaders of the Jan. 6 congressional committee on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Surgeon general calls for cancer warnings on alcohol
item.description: Alcoholic beverages should carry a prominent warning label about the link between alcohol and cancer, according to the U.S. surgeon general.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled this week the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) lacked the authority to restore certain net neutrality rules last year, handing a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden administration officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: New Year's attacks stoke fears of political violence
item.description: A pair of fatal attacks on New Year's Day have reignited concerns over political violence in the U.S. after an election already marked by multiple assassination attempts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to award medals of honor, valor in waning days at White House
item.description: President Biden will award the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers and the Medal of Valor to eight public safety officers at the White House on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Orca that carried her dead calf for weeks in 2018 is doing it again
item.description: An endangered Pacific Northwest orca that made global headlines in 2018 for carrying her dead calf for over two weeks is doing so once again following the death of her new calf, in another sign of grief over lost offspring, researchers said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal courts won't refer Clarence Thomas to attorney general over ethics
item.description: Thomas has agreed to follow updated requirements on reporting trips and gifts, including clearer guidelines on hospitality from friends.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 119th Congress Latest: Mike Johnson narrowly reelected House speaker
item.description: The 119th Congress is convening for the first time and House Speaker Mike Johnson has narrowly been reelected to his post.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Carter's quest for Mideast peace didn't end with Camp David
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — As president, Jimmy Carter brokered the watershed peace agreement that removed Israel’s most powerful enemy from the battlefield. But he incurred the Israeli government's wrath decades later when he said its military rule over the Palestinians amounted to apartheid.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man accused of choking reporter in racially-motivated attack
item.description: "The allegations in this case are very concerning," District Court Deputy DA Victoria Fazzino said. "The people have victim-safety concerns (and) community safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, WFLA, NBC

item.title: John Capodice, actor who appeared in 'Ace Ventura,' 'Seinfeld' and more, dies at 83
item.description: News of Capodice’s death on Monday was publicly announced in an obituary posted by the Pizzi Funeral Home in Northvale, New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to hold DC rally day before inauguration
item.description: The president-elect has not held a rally in the District of Columbia since he delivered a speech on the Ellipse on Jan. 6, 2021, which preceded a violent attack on the Capitol by supporters attempting to stop the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: FBI finds 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home in one of the largest such seizures, prosecutors say
item.description: The FBI said it believes it's the largest amount of homemade explosives it has encountered in its history
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sugar Bowl in New Orleans postponed after deadly Bourbon Street attack
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, KRON4

item.title: New York employers must now offer paid medical leave during pregnancy
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Pregnant New Yorkers will be entitled to at least 20 hours of paid leave to attend prenatal medical appointments under a law that took effect Wednesday. Gov. Kathy Hochul said the policy makes New York the first state in the country to offer paid leave for prenatal care.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 things that will shape US politics in 2025
item.description: The fallout of last month’s high-stakes election and recent battles within Congress are offering early clues of what will set the tone for politics in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Have any New Year's resolutions? Here are some tips to help make them stick
item.description: Studies have shown that up to 70% of people who make New Year’s resolutions abandon those good intentions within months. Here are some tips for how to keep those resolutions when your willpower starts to falter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch a replay of the 2025 Rose Parade presented by Honda
item.description: LA's Very Own KTLA 5 is proud to offer this replay of the 136th Rose Parade presented by Honda from New Year’s Day, Jan. 1, 2025. The theme of the 136th Rose Parade was “Best Day Ever!” Billie Jean King, a sports icon and equality champion, was the parade's Grand Marshal.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: Former Florida deputy identified as suspect in 1979 cold case, death of his wife: sheriff
item.description: A former deputy has been identified as the suspect in a 1979 cold case that left a 25-year-old store clerk dead, the Charlotte County Sheriff's Office announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: NYE drone show canceled on Folly Beach after drones fall from sky; at least 1 injured
item.description: FOLLY BEACH, S.C. (WCBD) - A new drone show on Folly Beach was canceled Tuesday night after the drones malfunctioned and fell from the sky. Organizers completed two drone displays before they all went down, News 2 learned.
item.dc:creator: Tim Renaud, Fox 5

item.title: Here are 5 hot-button laws taking effect in 2025
item.description: States across the country are bracing for new laws set to take effect as early as Wednesday, including some that have stirred controversy.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Multiple people injured in balloon-drop incident at Massachusetts arcade
item.description: Cheers of excitement quickly changed to horror when the netted balloons pulled a large display down onto the crowd below, including multiple children, Storyful reports.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: PlayStations for guns are offered up in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Dozens of cars lined up outside a New Orleans church parking lot this week as residents flocked to exchange firearms for video game consoles through a city-supported gun buyback initiative meant to improve public safety as Louisiana loosens gun ownership laws.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FDA testing aged raw cow's milk cheese for bird flu
item.description: Raw, unpasteurized milk is a known vector of bird flu when derived from infected cow herds. It can also contain pathogens such as E. coli, salmonella, and both Streptococcus and Staphylococcus bacteria.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Why you should probably throw away your Christmas tree this week
item.description: With the holiday season now behind us, it might be time to consider losing your Christmas trees, too.
item.dc:creator: Tylor Sorensen, Fox 5

item.title: Woman burned to death in NYC subway identified by police
item.description: The woman was set on fire and killed while likely asleep on a stationary train at the Stillwell Avenue subway station on Dec. 22, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, Fox 5

item.title: Australia, New Zealand first to welcome 2025 with fireworks
item.description: The U.S. countdown is still hours away, but those in New Zealand have already started 2025 with a bang.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US this New Year's Eve
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year. The sun expelled two bursts of plasma that are hurtling toward Earth and are expected to arrive early this week, according to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Korea struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
